key_insight: A personalized soft robotic exoglove can combine mobility assistance with massage-like compression to help reduce hand spasticity and pain.
engineering_relevance: This work shows how finite element analysis and custom soft actuator design can be used to fit complex hand anatomy while targeting both force delivery and comfort.
method: Designed dorsal, ventral, and palmar soft pneumatic actuators tailored to hand topology and kinematics.
Used finite element modeling at 100 and 200 kPa to assess stress and guide geometry optimization before fabrication.
Fabricated and assembled actuators into a wearable exoglove, then performed initial pilot-user testing.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful conformal contact, compression fit for a hyperflexed finger, stereolithography fabrication of a palmar actuator, and wearable validation with a pilot user.
watch_item: The results are preliminary and based on early prototype and pilot testing, so clinical effectiveness and long-term durability remain unproven.
paper_title: Towards Soft Robotic Exogloves for Musculoskeletal Manipulation to Reduce Pain and Spasticity
